verdict_reason: The verse's most striking commentarial divergence (Shankara's śruti = 'Veda' vs Ramanuja's śruti = 'hearing') is recorded faithfully in the source pack. The engineering body simplifies toward Shankara's reading (the more common one) but explicitly attributes it to Shankara, not to 'tradition' generically. The layered niścalā/acalā distinction matches Shankara's gloss; the source pack also records Ramanuja's eka-vṛtti gloss. Neither is misattributed. The PIVOT character of the verse (closing karma-yoga, opening sthita-prajña) is a structural-textual claim, not a doctrinal one — both Shankara and Ramanuja agree on the structural arc. Pass.
